component.name: RustyLens
component.summary: Lightweight OCR tool for the Linux desktop

description.p: RustyLens is a lightweight, open-source OCR tool for the Linux desktop. Open an image or capture a screenshot, and RustyLens extracts the text using Tesseract — with bounding-box overlays and drag-to-select word copying, similar to Google Lens.

ul.li: OCR text extraction with support for 100+ languages
Drag-to-select words on the image with Ctrl+C to copy
Screenshot capture mode via XDG Desktop Portal
Language selector with auto-detection of installed Tesseract packs

description.p: Flatpak CI fix and code quality improvements.

ul.li: Vendor Rust dependencies before flatpak-builder to fix offline Flatpak builds
Refactor let-chain patterns to Rust 2024 syntax in portal and UI code

description.p: Fix Flatpak and AppImage CI builds (remove --offline flag and redundant rename step).
Fix AppImage rename step when file already has correct name.
Fix Flatpak and AppImage CI builds.
Fix CI build and vendored dependency configuration.
Improved word selection, Flatpak build fixes, and GNOME 49 runtime upgrade.

ul.li: Replace drag-to-select rectangle with anchor-based range selection (Ctrl+C to copy)
Upgrade Flatpak runtime from GNOME 46 to GNOME 49
Fix Flatpak build: add LLVM SDK extension for libclang
Add Flathub-ready metadata (desktop file, metainfo, icon)
Add AppImage build support in CI
